evals.prompt: My controller is catching a database error and returning the raw Postgres error message to the client.
evals.expected_output: Violation: leaking raw DB errors to API. Fix: (1) Wrap DB error in infrastructure layer with context. (2) Map to domain error (e.g., ResourceNotFoundError). (3) API layer maps to HTTP 404 with standardized JSON envelope: { error: { code: 'USER_NOT_FOUND', message: '...' } }. Never expose stack traces.

evals.prompt: Design an error handling strategy for a layered Go service with HTTP API, domain logic, and database access.
evals.expected_output: Three-layer strategy: (1) Infra layer: wrap 3rd-party errors with fmt.Errorf('process: %w', err). (2) Domain layer: throw pure business errors (OrderPaymentFailedError) — NO HTTP codes here. (3) API layer: global error handler maps domain errors to HTTP responses. Error codes: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

evals.prompt: I have multiple catch blocks that just silently ignore errors: catch(e) {}
evals.expected_output: Violation: swallowing errors. Never use empty catch blocks. Fix: always log, handle, or rethrow. Options: (1) Log and rethrow: logger.error(e); throw e. (2) Transform: throw new AppError('OPERATION_FAILED', { cause: e }). (3) Return error type (Go-style). An empty catch hides bugs and makes debugging impossible.

should_not_trigger: Write a React component with a loading spinner
Explain the difference between TCP and UDP
